Reserves held in US Treasury Bills, government money market funds, and overnight US Treasury repurchase agreements via Circle Reserve Fund (BlackRock-managed). Conducted under AICPA attestation standards.

disclosures.title: Paxos Global Dollar (USDG) — Reserve Attestation April 2026
disclosures.summary: Monthly attestation by KPMG LLP (ISCA standards; effective 2026-02-27, replacing Enrome LLP) confirming Global Dollar (USDG) reserve assets equal or exceed USDG tokens in circulation. Issued by Paxos Digital Singapore Pte. Ltd. under MAS Major Payments Institution (MPI) licence (full licence granted 2024-07-01); DBS Bank is the primary banking partner. Reserves held 100% in USD cash deposits and short-term U.S. Treasury bills, segregated and bankruptcy-remote. USDG is the consortium currency of the Global Dollar Network (GDN), counting 130+ institutional partners as of May 2026 (founding members: Robinhood, Kraken, Galaxy, Anchorage, Bullish; subsequent additions include Mastercard, OKX, Gemini, DBS, Worldpay). Yield flows exclusively to GDN consortium members proportional to minting and volume contribution — retail token holders receive no yield. USDG additionally holds MiCA authorisation via Finnish FIN-FSA (effective July 2025). Circulation reached ~$2.75B within 18 months of launch (November 2024).

key_figures.label: Yield model
key_figures.value: 100% of reserve income to GDN partners; retail holders receive no yield

disclosures.title: Paxos Dollar (USDP) — Reserve Attestation April 2026
disclosures.summary: Monthly attestation by KPMG LLP (effective 2025-02-28, replacing WithumSmith+Brown uniformly across all Paxos products) confirming Paxos Dollar (USDP) reserve assets equal or exceed USDP tokens in circulation. Issued by Paxos Trust Company, LLC under NYDFS Limited Purpose Trust Charter. Reserves held 100% in cash and cash equivalents: USD deposits at FDIC-insured banks and short-duration U.S. Treasury bills (under 90 days), held in segregated, bankruptcy-remote accounts. USDP circulation has declined significantly from a ~$1B+ peak in 2022–2023 to approximately $32–41M as of mid-2026. Paxos no longer proactively publishes monthly USDP reserve reports; attestations are available on request (press@paxos.com). No publicly accessible static PDF link — document served via JavaScript-rendered transparency page.

disclosures.title: Tether USD (USDT) — ISAE 3000R Assurance Report 31 December 2025
disclosures.summary: ISAE 3000R assurance report prepared by BDO Advisory Services S.r.l. (Milan, Italy) providing a point-in-time opinion on Tether International S.A. de C.V.'s reserves as of 31 December 2025. Issuing entity was redomiciled from BVI to El Salvador in January 2025 and holds a Stablecoin Issuer authorisation from El Salvador's CNAD. Total reserves of $192.9B exceed liabilities of $186.5B (approx. 103.4% collateralised). Reserve composition: US Treasury Bills ($122.3B, 63.4%), overnight and term reverse repurchase agreements ($24.8B, 12.9%), precious metals ($17.5B, 9.0%), secured loans ($17.0B, 8.8%), and Bitcoin ($8.4B, 4.4%). Report commissioned by Tether Global Investments Fund SICAF SA under ISAE 3000 (Revised); BDO does not express an audit opinion on financial statements.

disclosures.title: Tether Gold (XAUT) — ISAE 3000R Assurance Report 31 December 2025
disclosures.summary: ISAE 3000R assurance report by BDO Italia S.p.A. (Milan) confirming Tether Gold (XAUT) reserve assets as of 31 December 2025. Issued by TG Commodities, S.A. de C.V. (El Salvador; redomiciled from BVI January 2025), a separate entity from Tether International within the Tether Group. Each XAUT token is backed 1:1 by one fine troy ounce of LBMA Good Delivery gold bar. Physical gold (~520,089 troy oz, approximately 16.2 metric tons) is stored in segregated vaults in Switzerland (custodians: MKS PAMP and Loomis — MED confidence; verify from PDF cover). XAUT supply grew approximately 107% during H2 2025 (from ~246K to ~520K oz), driven by the gold price rally. Quarterly attestation cadence: Q1 2026 report not yet published as of June 2026. Bar-level transparency: each XAUT token is traceable to a specific gold bar serial number on-chain. Note: verify exact issuing entity name on PDF cover (TG Commodities vs. Tether Holdings S.A. de C.V.).

Peg

disclosures.title: Mountain Protocol USDM — Final Reserve Attestation 23 October 2025
disclosures.summary: Final accountant's report by Nephos Group (UK, ATA-registered; signing accountant: Joseph David) attesting to the reserve composition of Mountain Protocol's USDM stablecoin. USDM was backed 100% by short-duration US Treasury Bills (average duration ≤ 60 days) and distributed daily yield via an on-chain rebasing mechanism (rewardMultiplier), with historical APY tracking US T-Bill rates (approximately 4–5%). Mountain Protocol holds BMA Digital Asset Business Licence No. 202302512 (Bermuda). The protocol initiated a three-phase wind-down in May 2025: yield reduced to 0% from June 12, primary market closed August 22, 2025, and remaining reserves migrated on-chain to a Uniswap AMM pool. This October 2025 report is the final third-party attestation; post–Phase 3 reserve verification is on-chain only. Product is no longer operational.

disclosures.title: Hashnote US Yield Coin (USYC) — Product Structure Disclosure (Post-Circle Acquisition)
disclosures.summary: Hashnote International Short Duration Yield Fund Ltd. (Cayman Islands, CIMA-registered mutual fund, LEI 25490083NU09WKP5Z570) issues USYC tokens representing short-duration U.S. Treasury bill and overnight reverse repo exposure. Circle Internet Group acquired Hashnote Holdings LLC effective January 21, 2025 (~$99.8M); token administration transferred to Circle International Bermuda Limited (CIBL), regulated by the Bermuda Monetary Authority. DRW (Cumberland) remains strategic liquidity partner. Annual fund audit by Valaston International Limited (CIMA-approved specialist Cayman fund auditor). No public attestation PDF is released — transparency is provided via on-chain daily NAV oracle; fund documents available on request to qualified investors only. Eligible investors: non-U.S. persons (Reg S). As of mid-2026, USYC AUM is approximately $2.957B, making it the world’s largest tokenized money market fund. Performance fee: 10% of yield (verify against offering docs).

disclosures.arranger: Bank of China (HK) · Crédit Agricole CIB · Goldman Sachs (Asia) · HSBC · ICBC (Asia) · UBS
disclosures.title: HKSAR Government — Phase 2 Multi-currency Digital Green Bond (February 2024)
disclosures.summary: On 7 February 2024, the HKSAR Government priced the world's first multi-currency digital green bond, raising approximately HK$6 billion equivalent across four currency tranches: HK$2 billion at 3.800%, RMB 1.5 billion at 2.900%, USD 200 million at 4.749%, and EUR 80 million at 3.647% — all 2-year tenor, matured 7 February 2026. Legal title to the bonds was maintained in dematerialised form on the HSBC Orion distributed ledger platform, operated jointly with the HKMA's Central Moneymarkets Unit (CMU), enabling dual-rail settlement. Six joint lead managers co-arranged: Bank of China (HK), Crédit Agricole CIB, Goldman Sachs (Asia), HSBC, ICBC (Asia), and UBS. Proceeds are earmarked under the Government Green Bond Framework; Vigeo Eiris provided the Second Party Opinion. This is the second HKSAR Government digital green bond (the inaugural was issued in February 2023 on Goldman Sachs's GS DAP platform) and the first standardised application of Bond Data Taxonomy elements in Hong Kong.

key_figures.label: Settlement platform
key_figures.value: HSBC Orion DLT + CMU dual-rail · first multi-currency digital bond globally

disclosures.title: Superstate Short Duration US Government Securities Fund — Initial Reg D Form D Filing 2024
disclosures.summary: Superstate Short Duration US Government Securities Fund is a Delaware statutory trust series (Superstate Asset Trust) issuing USTB tokens under Regulation D Rule 506(c), Section 3(c)(7), restricted to U.S. qualified purchasers. The fund holds exclusively short-duration U.S. Treasury bills and government agency securities (~28-day weighted average maturity, no repos). Annual audits are conducted by Ernst & Young LLP; custodian is UMB Bank N.A. (current product page references BNY Mellon — custodian transition unconfirmed). In March 2026, Superstate announced transfer of investment management to Invesco Advisers, Inc.; upon completion (expected Q2 2026) the fund will be renamed ‘Invesco Short Duration US Government Securities Fund’ while retaining USTB smart contracts. Superstate continues as on-chain technology partner. This entry references the EDGAR Form D (CIK 2004367, 2024 filing). Initial Form D filing date 2024 (exact day TBD; EDGAR access blocked at research time, to be verified manually).

disclosures.title: Goldfinch Protocol — Senior Pool Disclosure & Default History (2021–2023)
disclosures.summary: The Goldfinch Senior Pool is a permissionless USDC on-chain lending pool deploying capital to Borrower Pools at a 4:1 algorithmic leverage ratio (85% Senior, 15% junior backer first-loss). Built by Warbler Labs (San Francisco, US) and launched January 2021, the protocol funded uncollateralised loans to Global South fintechs — motorcycle financing (Tugende, Kenya), emerging market SME credit (Cauris, Almavest), and fintech credit funds (Stratos). Three defaults materialised between 2022 and 2023: Tugende (~$5M default, ~3.95% NAV write-down to Senior Pool); Stratos ($20M loan / ~$7M loss following portfolio investments in REZI and POKT); Lend East (~$5.9M loss). The October 2023 Stratos update (Warbler Labs governance disclosure) is the primary formal public communication about the defaults and Senior Pool exposure. In February 2025 Warbler Labs launched Goldfinch Prime — institutional private credit access via Apollo, Ares, and Golub (targeting 9–12% net returns) — as the successor product. Senior Pool on-chain TVL is approximately $1.6M as of mid-2026 (DefiLlama), reflecting substantial wind-down from peak. No PDF disclosure; primary disclosures are on-chain (app.goldfinch.finance) and governance forum (gov.goldfinch.finance).

disclosures.title: Ondo OUSG (Ondo I LP) — Initial Reg D Form D Filing January 2023
disclosures.summary: Ondo I LP is a Delaware limited partnership issuing OUSG tokens under Regulation D Rule 506(c), Section 3(c)(7), restricted to U.S. qualified purchasers with a $5,000 minimum. The fund allocates principally to BlackRock’s BUIDL institutional money market fund alongside secondary positions in FedFund (TFDXX), Franklin Templeton, WisdomTree, and Fidelity government vehicles — resulting in layered counterparty exposure across Ondo and BlackRock structures. Daily NAV is calculated by NAV Consulting; off-chain assets are held at Clear Street LLC (qualified custodian) and Coinbase Custody for digital assets. Annual financial audits are performed and delivered to limited partners, but the audit firm is not publicly named. No public attestation PDF or factsheet is released. This entry references the EDGAR Form D filing (CIK 1957431, filed January 11, 2023).

key_figures.label: Primary holding
key_figures.value: BlackRock BUIDL (significant majority); secondary: FedFund (TFDXX), Franklin Templeton, WisdomTree, Fidelity

disclosures.title: BlockTower Credit Series I–IV — Centrifuge Pool Onboarding Proposal (December 2022)
disclosures.summary: BlockTower Credit (private structured credit arm of BlockTower Capital) launched four tokenized credit pools on Centrifuge's Tinlake protocol in December 2022, backed by a $220M commitment comprising $150M in senior DAI from MakerDAO (via direct deposit vault, DROP token at fixed 4% APR) and $70M junior equity from BlockTower (TIN token, first-loss buffer). The underlying assets are consumer asset-backed securities (ABS), auto ABS, and CLO tranches managed by BlockTower Credit through a Cayman Islands SPV. Series 4 (Ethereum pool address 0x55d86d51ac3bcab7ab7d2124931fba106c8b60c7) had approximately $56M in DAI supply as of November 2023. The initiative achieved a 97% reduction in securitization costs compared to traditional structured finance infrastructure, as documented in the Centrifuge case study. The Pool Onboarding Proposal (POP) — approved via on-chain governance at gov.centrifuge.io — is the primary formal issuance disclosure for this program. Pools are on the legacy Tinlake v1 protocol; migration status to Centrifuge v2 should be verified given MakerDAO's 2024–2025 Sky restructuring of RWA positions.

disclosures.title: M11 Credit USDC Pool — Orthogonal Trading Default Disclosure (December 2022)
disclosures.summary: M11 Credit's USDC pool on Maple Finance became the focal point of the largest DeFi institutional credit default of 2022 when Orthogonal Trading — a crypto market maker with $31M outstanding across four active loans in the pool — failed to repay a $10M tranche on December 4, 2022. Maple Finance severed ties with Orthogonal on December 5, 2022, alleging repeated misrepresentation of FTX exchange exposure throughout November 2022: Orthogonal had claimed only $2.5M in FTX exposure before reversing its position on December 3. M11 Credit (pool delegate; part of the Dutch Maven 11 Group) issued a formal default notice for all active Orthogonal loans. Investors in the M11 USDC pool faced approximately 80% losses on remaining capital; M11 WETH pool investors faced 17% losses from a concurrent $5M Orthogonal position. Total Orthogonal defaults on Maple reached $36M across 8 loans. Combined with other 2022 defaults including Auros Global, total Maple bad debt reached approximately $54M. Recovery efforts engaged Kroll for asset recovery; Orthogonal Trading was placed into BVI provisional liquidation on December 22, 2022. This event directly triggered Maple Finance's protocol overhaul from v1 to v2, introduction of mandatory borrower KYC, and launch of the Treasury-backed Cash Management pool.

key_figures.label: Orthogonal Trading default (December 5, 2022)
key_figures.value: $36M total across 8 loans (M11 USDC pool: $31M / 4 loans; M11 WETH pool: $5M)

disclosures.title: European Investment Bank — Inaugural Digital Bond on Ethereum (April 2021)
disclosures.summary: On 27 April 2021, the European Investment Bank (EIB) issued its first-ever digital bond on a public blockchain: €100 million, 2-year, 0.000% coupon (re-offer yield −0.601%; re-offer price 101.213%), governed by French law, settling 28 April 2021. The bond matured 28 April 2023. Joint lead managers were Goldman Sachs, Santander, and Société Générale, with Société Générale FORGE acting as registrar, fiscal agent, and settlement platform manager on the Ethereum public blockchain. Settlement of underwriting proceeds was represented on-chain as a central bank digital currency (CBDC) in a Banque de France pilot — the first primary bond issuance to use a CBDC settlement leg. Legal counsel: Linklaters LLP (EIB) and Allen & Overy LLP (joint lead managers). The issuance is the first multi-dealer-led, primary-market digitally native token by a AAA-rated issuer on a public blockchain, establishing Ethereum as credible infrastructure for institutional bond primary markets.

key_figures.label: Settlement
key_figures.value: Ethereum public blockchain; settlement proceeds as Banque de France CBDC (first CBDC bond settlement leg)

key_figures.label: Platform / registrar
key_figures.value: Société Générale FORGE — registrar, fiscal agent, settlement platform manager

disclosures.title: Société Générale SFH — OFH Security Token (Covered Bond) on Ethereum (April 2019)
disclosures.summary: On 18 April 2019, Société Générale SFH — the covered bond subsidiary of Société Générale Group — issued €100 million of obligations de financement de l'habitat (OFH) as security tokens directly registered on the Ethereum public blockchain, the first covered bond ever issued as a blockchain security token by a commercial bank. Rated Aaa by Moody's and AAA by Fitch. The OFH tokens were fully subscribed by Société Générale itself (not distributed to external investors), structuring this as a proof-of-concept pilot for DLT-based covered bond issuance workflows. SG Forge — then an internal innovation startup within Société Générale's intrapreneurial programme — operated as the technology platform. Legal advisor: Gide Loyrette Nouel; blockchain advisor: PwC France. Coupon rate and maturity date are not publicly disclosed in any available issuance document. The transaction predates the EIB's April 2021 public digital bond by two years and established the template for commercial bank tokenised debt on permissionless Ethereum. Maturity date not publicly disclosed in any available issuer document; status conservatively marked retired given the 2019 issue date and typical OFH covered bond tenor of 5–10 years.

key_figures.value: OFH — obligations de financement de l'habitat (French covered bond); issuer: Société Générale SFH
